rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH QUINN ET AL VS. MARY R. COULTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT, ETC.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 10, 2024 line 1. Defendants Stein and Staghalis Motion for Determination of Good Faith Settlement is GRANTED. Moving party met the Tech-Built factors. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARY GEAN ET AL VS. EDMUND GEAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appraisal And Appraiser Appointment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 10, 2024 line 2. Defendant's Motion for Appraisal. Hearing Required. The parties are ordered to meet and confer before the hearing and submit a joint brief no later than 9:00 a.m. on September 10, 2024 indicating whether (1) the parties agree on the value of the property; and if "yes," indicate such agreed-upon value; and (2) whether the parties agreed on "another method of valuation," and if "yes," specify such method. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARY GEAN ET AL VS. EDMUND GEAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of His Fifth Cause Of Action For Partition Contained In His First Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 10, 2024 line 2. Cross-Complainant's Motion for Summary Adjudication of his Fifth Cause of Action for Partition is DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). Compare Notice of Motion and Separate Statement. The motion fails to dispose of the entire cause of action for partition as pled in the operative Cross-Complaint. See prayer for relief. The motion is premature in light of CCP 874.316. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. KENNETH HUGHES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion, Motion, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration In Support Of Motion To Vacate Default And Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 10, 2024 line 6. Defendant's Motion to Vacate Default/Judgment is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden and show mistake, surprise or excusable neglect. Defendant does not deny personal service on June 26, 2024. Defendant does not explain what steps, if any, defendant took to address the complaint, until Sheriff's Notice was posted for August 28, 2024 eviction, i.e. two months later. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
